title: Implement event tailer and fleet watch command
description: Build the merged `.takt/logs/events.jsonl` tailer with optional replay window parsing and the `watch` command that streams project-tagged events from multiple projects concurrently.
acceptance_criteria: Tailing uses one polling worker per project plus a merged output queue as described in the spec.
`--since` replays events from the requested duration window before continuing live from EOF.
Short-history warnings and unparseable timestamp handling follow the spec's best-effort behaviour.
`takt-fleet watch` prints project-prefixed merged events and exits cleanly on Ctrl-C.

handoff_summary.completed: Created `src/agent_takt_fleet/tailer.py` with `TailedEvent` dataclass, `_parse_timestamp`, `_emit_line`, `_replay_window` (with cutoff filtering, short-history warnings, and unparseable-timestamp best-effort), `_do_tail` (waits for file, replays window or seeks to EOF, live-polls), `_tail_worker` (thread wrapper with None sentinel), and `start_tailing` (one daemon thread per project, single merged queue). Created `src/agent_takt_fleet/cli/commands/watch.py` with `command_watch` that loads/filters the registry, calls `start_tailing`, drains the merged queue to stdout until Ctrl-C (sets stop_event, returns 0). Added `format_watch_event_line` to `formatters.py` — project-prefixed with timestamp, event type, and summary for parsed events; raw fallback for unparseable lines. Created `tests/fleet/test_tailer.py` (15 tests: timestamp parsing, emit_line, replay_window with cutoff/warning/stop-event/unparseable, start_tailing with multiple projects/live events/file-wait). Created `tests/fleet/test_cli_watch.py` (9 tests: empty registry, registry error, no-match filter, event output, multi-event, Ctrl-C, --since forwarding, project filter, raw unparseable line).
handoff_summary.risks: The `_build_mock_start_tailing` helper in test_cli_watch.py pushes one sentinel per project; a single-threaded test environment might see timing differences but all sentinels are in-queue synchronously so there is no race. File rotation is not handled — the tailer re-opens the same path; rotation would require inode tracking which is out of scope.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should run the fleet test suite and verify all tailer and watch tests pass.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Downstream tester and review agents should be aware that `formatters.py` was extended (not modified at existing lines) so no conflicts with sibling beads that only read formatters.
handoff_summary.design_decisions:
**Polling vs. inotify**: Used polling (1-second interval) for cross-platform simplicity. inotify/FSEvents would need platform detection and adds dependency complexity for no meaningful latency improvement in this use case.

**File open mode (text vs binary)**: Opened in text mode (`"r"`) for simpler line iteration. Binary mode would require explicit decoding but adds no value for JSONL polling.

**Replay then live-tail in a single open()**: The replay window reads from position 0 to EOF, then the live-tail loop continues from the same file handle positioned at EOF. This avoids a second open and a potential TOCTOU gap between reading historical events and starting the live tail.

**None sentinel per worker**: Each worker pushes `None` when it exits (normal stop or error). `command_watch` counts sentinels to know when all workers are done — this makes the loop naturally finite after stop_event is set.

**Short-history warning**: Emitted to stderr (not stdout) so it doesn't interfere with the event stream being piped. Condition: `first_ts_in_file > cutoff`.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) replay window correctly excludes events before cutoff across boundary conditions; (2) short-history warning fires when log is shorter than the requested `--since` window; (3) unparseable JSON lines are included best-effort in replay; (4) live-tail picks up events appended after file position; (5) Ctrl-C exits with code 0 and `(interrupted)` message; (6) `--since` value is forwarded to `start_tailing`; (7) project filter reduces the worker count; (8) RegistryError returns exit code 1.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: File rotation is not handled — if `events.jsonl` is rotated while the tailer is running, the old file descriptor continues reading from the old inode until stop_event is set. This is acceptable for the v0.1 scope. The tailer does not track bead context (bead_id) in event output; the raw event summary is shown as-is.

conflict_risks: Downstream tester and review agents should be aware that `formatters.py` was extended (not modified at existing lines) so no conflicts with sibling beads that only read formatters.

execution_history.summary: Implemented event tailer (`tailer.py`) and fleet watch command (`cli/commands/watch.py`), plus `format_watch_event_line` in `formatters.py`, with full test suites for both.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
